#  Resource Tracking And Unit Quantity Based Budget Setup Guide 
Table of Contents 
##  Add Custom Segments 
###  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Admin** tool.
  2. Under 'Company Settings', click the **Work Breakdown Structure** link.
  3. In the 'Segments' table, click the **Add Segment** button. 
![Wbs Default Segment Click Add](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/25fe5c91cbe1f34295eda4b3709182fb/22a034ace515ba29f3e99e423c386464/wbs-default-segment-click-add.png?fm=webp)
  4. In the 'New Segment' window, do the following: 
#####  Note
An asterisk (*) in the list below indicates a required field.
![Wbs New Segment](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/29dd508f254e2865744c3da1c5e6be47/128535c82fb650bf995020fe91bee0f0/create_new_custom_segment.png?fm=webp)
     * **Segment Name***. Enter a name for your new segment. This is a required field. Duplicate segment names are NOT permitted. A 'Segment Name is in use' message appears to notify if you attempt to enter a name that already exists. 
#####  Tip
**Not sure what custom segments to create?** To learn how other Procore clients use Work Breakdown Structure, see [What custom segments should we create for our company's Work Breakdown Structure?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-custom-segments-should-we-create-for-our-companys-work-breakdown-structure)
     * **Segment Structure***. Select a value from the drop-down list. This is a required field. Your choices include:
       * **Flat**. Select this option to add your segment to the list with no hierarchy.
       * **Tiered**. Select this option to nest your segment into a tree (hierarchical) structure. 
#####  Tip
**What's the difference between a flat and tiered segment?** To learn the difference, see [What is the difference between a flat and tiered segment in Procore's WBS?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-difference-between-a-flat-and-tiered-segment-in-procores-wbs)
  5. Under the **Project Level Actions** section, choose one (1) or all of these options: 
#####  Note
When the 'Project Level Actions' settings are turned ON, your project administrators (users with 'Admin' level permissions on the Project level Admin tool) are permitted to perform specific tasks on segments and segment items at the project level. These settings are turned OFF by default.
     * **Add/Edit/Delete Project Level Segment Items.** Place a mark in this checkbox to provide project administrators with the ability to add, edit, and delete unused segment items on a project. This setting is turned OFF by default. 
#####  Note
When this setting is turned ON, project administrators can perform these tasks:
       * [Add Segment Items to a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/project-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/add-segment-items)
       * [Edit Segment Items on a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/project-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/edit-segment-items)
       * [Delete Unused Segment Items from a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/project-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/delete-unused-segment-items)
     * **Delete Segment Items Inherited from Company.** Place a mark in this checkbox to provide project administrators with the ability to delete segment items inherited at the Project level from the Company level Admin tool. This is useful to project administrators who don't want new segment items created at the company level to be used on a project. See [Delete Unused Segment Items from a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/project-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/delete-unused-segment-items).
     * **Make segment required when creating Budget Codes.** Place a mark in this checkbox to require this segment for new Budget Codes.
  6. Click **Create**. Procore adds the new segment to the bottom of the 'Segments' list.
  7. Repeat the steps above for all of the new segments to add.


#####  Tips
  * **Need to edit your new segment's settings?** The best time to edit a new segment is before you start using it on a project. Once a custom segment is used to create a project budget code, you can edit it at any time. For details, see [Edit Custom Segment Settings](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/company-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/edit-custom-segment-settings).
  * **Want to delete a custom segment?** Its important to note that a custom segment cannot be deleted after it has been added to the budget code structure on one (1) or more projects in your company's Procore account. To learn more, see [Delete Custom Segments](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/company-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/delete-custom-segments).
  * **Want to add segment items?** If your new segment is created, you can add new items now. For instructions, see [Add Custom Segment Items](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/company-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/add-segment-items).
  * **Ready to define your company's default budget code structure?** You can arrange your segments in any order you want. This arrangement defines your company's budget code structure. For instructions, see [Arrange Segments to Form the Company's Budget Code Structure](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/company-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/set-company-budget-code-structure).
